title: 10 best herbal creams for oily hair

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of sage work to reduce excess sebum secretion, leaving the hair feeling clean and manageable. Additionally, the herb's astringent qualities help to tighten the pores, removing impurities and product buildup that can exacerbate oily locks. As a result, sage creams provide a natural solution for controlling oiliness and promoting a healthy, balanced scalp-hair complex.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and lead to oily hair by reducing sebum production.

Astringent properties: Sage's astringent compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help tighten the hair follicle and reduce oil secretion, controlling excess sebum and promoting balanced hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, including camphor and borneol, soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flaking associated with oily hair, while also helping to regulate sebum production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oil production.
Barks: Containing tannins and other compounds that reduce inflammation, barks can help to balance the skin's natural pH and regulate sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in early morning misty weather for maximum potency and essential oils.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or until crispy and brittle.
Combine 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a glass jar for infusion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily to allow for gentle maceration and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discard solids and add 10% beeswax for emulsification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The herbs in lemon balm cream, such as Melissa officinalis, work to absorb excess sebum and reduce shine, leaving hair looking healthier and more manageable. Additionally, lemon balm's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to soothe an itchy scalp and prevent dandruff, further reducing the appearance of oily roots and flyaways.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm cream's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: The astringent and antiseptic properties of lemon balm cream help to dry up excess oil, reduce inflammation, and prevent further oil buildup by constricting hair follicles and reducing sebum production.

Calming and soothing properties: Lemon balm's calming and soothing properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related scalp irritation, which can contribute to oily hair, while also providing relief from itchiness and flakiness associated with dandruff or other scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots help soothe and calm scalp irritation associated with oily hair.
Leaves: High in essential oils and flavonoids, lemon balm leaves provide a gentle, non-greasy formula to regulate sebum production and balance the scalp's natural moisture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and dry them properly for future use.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis with 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks at room temperature allowing infusion to occur.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard solids before mixing with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer or spatula until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with oily hair because they contain natural soothing properties that help to regulate the scalp's oil production. The aloe vera gel absorbs excess moisture and reduces frizz, leaving the hair feeling lighter and more manageable. Additionally, aloe's anti-inflammatory properties calm irritated scalps caused by oily buildup, while its antifungal properties prevent fungal growth that can contribute to dandruff and flakiness. This results in a cleaner, healthier-looking mane with reduced oiliness and improved overall texture.
remedy_properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial properties: Help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and dandruff, by killing off bacteria that feed on sebum.
Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties: Soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and redness caused by excessive oil production, while also helping to regulate oil gland function.
Aloe vera's astringent properties: Help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil leakage and preventing further oil buildup, while also absorbing excess oil and impurities.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, making them effective in balancing scalp pH and controlling excess oil production.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps soothe and calm irritated scalps while reducing dandruff and flaking.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality aloe vera gel from 10-15 leaves of organic aloe vera plants.
Heat 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at low temperature for 30 minutes.
Steep 20g of dried calendula flowers and 15g of dried lavender buds in the heated oil for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding solids, and mix with 50ml of aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with oily hair because of its natural ability to balance oil production in the scalp. The herb's essential oils have astringent properties that help to tighten the pores, reducing sebum secretion and controlling excess oil. Additionally, rosemary's stimulating effects on blood circulation promote healthy hair growth, which can lead to a decrease in oiliness as the hair follicles become more balanced. Regular use of rosemary creams can leave oily hair looking healthy, manageable, and free from greasiness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oil production to increase, leading to healthier and less oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and irritation in the scalp, which can be caused by excess oil production, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.

Stimulating properties: Rosemary cream's stimulating properties increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to remove impurities and toxins that can contribute to oily hair, while also promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are used due to their ability to absorb and purify excess oil from the scalp, helping to balance sebum production and reduce greasiness.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are valued for their astringent properties, which help to tighten the pores on the scalp and minimize oil secretion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the herb-infused mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for effective extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the resulting infused oil with 10% beeswax and 5% vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth creamy texture and package immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower cream helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to oil buildup and other issues associated with oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flaking that can exacerbate oily hair conditions like dandruff or psoriasis.

Antibacterial properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oil buildup and other scalp issues; by controlling bacterial populations, the cream may help to regulate sebum production and reduce greasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling oil production and reducing dandruff in oily hair.
Buds: The buds of the purple coneflower are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean dried echinacea purpurea flowers weighing 2 ounces.
Combine the echinacea purpurea flowers with 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature for 4 hours under gentle stirring.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids before whipping it with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool before storing in an airtight container.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with oily hair because they contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing excess oil production. The cream also contains soothing aloe vera that calms irritated scalps, while its light, non-greasy texture absorbs quickly into the hair, leaving it feeling clean and refreshed. Additionally, purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce dandruff and itchiness, promoting healthy-looking locks with reduced oiliness.

English lavender creams helps with oily hair because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to balance the scalp's pH level, reducing excess oil production. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to regulate the hair follicles, leading to a reduction in sebum secretion. Additionally, lavender's ability to absorb excess moisture helps to dry out oily locks, leaving them feeling clean, fresh, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender cream helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to excessive oil production, promoting healthier and more balanced locks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling in the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by irritating the skin and causing it to produce even more oil.
Astringent properties: English lavender's astringent properties help to dry out and tighten pores on the scalp, reducing sebum production and minimizing the appearance of greasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help balance oil production and soothe scalp irritations.
Flowers: Calming and antiseptic properties of lavender flowers reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth on oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 24 hours to extract essential oils.
Combine infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until beeswax is fully melted after 10 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and lavender essential oil to the mixture, stirring well before cooling at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until it becomes light and fluffy, resembling whipped cream.
Transfer the finished cream to sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months for optimal shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with oily hair because they contain the potent herb, Calendula officinalis, which has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. These properties help to control excess oil production by reducing inflammation in the scalp and pores, resulting in a balanced moisture level. The cream also helps to purify the scalp, removing impurities and product buildup that can contribute to oily hair. This gentle, non-greasy treatment leaves hair looking healthy and manageable, with reduced frizz and flyaways.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by oily hair.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: The astringent and antiseptic properties of pot marigold cream help to reduce excess oil production on the scalp, while also preventing bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to oily hair.

Sudorific and diaphoretic properties: Pot marigold cream's sudorific and diaphoretic properties stimulate sweat glands, helping to open up the pores of the scalp and remove excess oil and impurities from the hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm oily scalp irritations.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, buds provide natural astringent and antiseptic properties to control sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to preserve them.
Combine dried calendula flowers with coconut oil and beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beeswax melts and emulsifies.
Remove from heat and let cool for 1 hour before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with oily hair because of its unique properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The cream works by reducing inflammation and itching, which can contribute to excessive oil secretion. It also helps to regulate the sebum glands, preventing them from overproducing oil and leaving your hair looking greasy. Additionally, stinging nettle has astringent properties that help to absorb excess oil, leaving your hair feeling clean and manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds like caffeic acid and scopoletin, which help reduce inflammation in the scalp, reducing redness and itching associated with oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle cream, such as beta-carotene and flavonoids, protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of oiliness.

Astringent properties: Stinging nettle contains a compound called caffeic acid, which acts as an astringent, helping to tighten pores on the scalp and reduce sebum production, leading to reduced oiliness and a healthier scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm oily scalp conditions.
Seeds: Nettle seeds contain essential fatty acids that nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality urtica dioica leaves, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the fresh urtica dioica leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours.
Grind dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ground urtica dioica with two parts coconut oil and three parts beeswax, heating the mixture until smooth and creamy.
Allow the herbal cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an air-tight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural astringent and antiseptic properties that work to balance the scalp's oil production. The creams absorb excess sebum, purifying the scalp and hair follicles, while also reducing dandruff and flaking. This results in a healthier, more manageable mane that looks and feels great. Additionally, dandelion creams often contain soothing agents that calm irritation and reduce redness, leaving your locks looking vibrant and full of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oil production, leading to a reduction in oily hair and scalp.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion cream can reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, which can be exacerbated by oily hair, promoting a healthier scalp environment.

Astringent properties: Dandelion's astringent properties help tighten pores, reducing oil production and absorption into the hair shaft, leading to a natural balance of sebum production and reduced greasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their natural absorbency, dandelion roots help to control excess oil production and balance the scalp's natural pH.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, dandelion seeds nourish the scalp and hair follicles, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale flowers, distilled water, coconut oil, and beeswax.
Combine 20 grams of dried flowers with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain and discard solids before mixing with 50 milliliters of melted coconut oil.
Add 10 grams of grated beeswax to the mixture and stir until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Pour the cooled mixture into tin containers, let it solidify, and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with oily hair because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp. The herbal extract has antiseptic qualities that reduce excess oil production by controlling the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to greasiness. Additionally, St John's wort contains flavonoids that have a clarifying effect on the hair shaft, leaving it feeling clean and refreshed while reducing frizz and flyaways associated with oily locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort has antioxidant compounds like hypericin and hyperforin, which help reduce free radical damage to the scalp and hair follicles, reducing inflammation and oil production.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extract contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and swelling, and thereby decreasing sebum production.

Astringent properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help reduce the appearance of pores and tighten the hair follicles, leading to less oil secretion and more balanced scalp hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain saponins and other compounds that help regulate oil production in the scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in calming irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried st john's wort flowers and organic beeswax in their recommended proportions.
Mix 200ml of coconut oil with 50g of dried st john's wort flowers in a heat-proof glass container.
Combine the infused oil mixture with 30g of melted organic beeswax and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and allow it to cool and solidify for approximately 2 hours.
Once set, store the finished cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance sebum production in the scalp. The herb's natural oils work to regulate the oil glands, reducing excess oil secretion and leaving hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Additionally, fennel's antiseptic properties help to purify the scalp, preventing clogged pores that can contribute to oily hair. By using a fennel-based cream regularly, individuals with oily hair can enjoy a cleaner, healthier looking mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to oil buildup and dandruff, promoting healthier and more balanced hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe irritated scalps, reduce redness and itching, and alleviate conditions like psoriasis and eczema that can exacerbate oily hair.

Natural astringent properties: Fennel's astringent properties help balance the scalp's natural pH, absorb excess oil, and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of greasiness and promoting a healthy shine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots help to balance oil production and soothe irritated scalps.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain volatile oils that help to purify the scalp and reduce excess sebum production, making them ideal for oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Grind 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil in a glass jar stirring vigorously for 2 minutes.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes under gentle stirring.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with oily hair because of its ability to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The licorice extract in these creams has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe an oily scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness. Additionally, licorice has antiseptic qualities that purify the scalp and hair follicles, removing impurities and excess oils. This leads to a reduction in oil production, resulting in healthier-looking, more manageable hair with less shine and frizz.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness and irritation caused by dandruff, itchy scalp, or other skin conditions associated with oily hair.
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial property of licorice cream helps to control the growth of yeast and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.

Sebum-regulating property: Licorice cream's sebum-regulating property helps to balance the production of sebum, an oily substance produced by the scalp, thus reducing excess oiliness and promoting healthy hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce sebum production and inflammation, making them effective in controlling oily hair.
Barks: Contain triterpenoids that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, useful in treating scalp irritations and infections associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Crush 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 100ml of distilled water to create a paste that is allowed to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before pouring into sterilized tubes or jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with oily hair because they possess unique properties that absorb excess oil and purify the scalp. The herbal ingredients in marshmallow creams, such as witch hazel and tea tree oil, work together to dry out the scalp and reduce sebum production, leaving hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Additionally, the cream's natural astringent properties help to tighten the pores, removing dirt and impurities that can contribute to oily buildup. As a result, marshmallow creams provide a natural solution for controlling oiliness and promoting healthy-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages, which are natural thickening agents that help to absorb excess oil and impurities from the scalp, leaving it feeling cleaner and more balanced.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow creams help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oily hair by disrupting the scalp's natural pH balance, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy sebum production.

Soothing properties: Marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated scalps, reduce flakiness and itchiness, and promote a healthy environment for hair growth, all of which can contribute to a reduction in oiliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated scalps associated with oily hair.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and irritation caused by oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis and dry it properly for future use.
Combine dried marshmallow root with distilled water in a ratio of one part herb to three parts liquid.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes at room temperature then strain it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix the herbal infusion with melted sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with oily hair because its unique blend of natural herbs and extracts works to balance the scalp's oil production. The antioxidants in beach rose help to neutralize excess sebum, while the astringent properties of witch hazel and tea tree oil tighten the pores and reduce shine. Additionally, the soothing effects of aloe vera calm irritated scalps, allowing for healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of greasiness. As a result, beach rose creams leave hair feeling lightweight, manageable, and refreshingly clean.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Beach rose cream's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and dandruff.
Astringent and Constricting Properties: The astringent and constricting properties of beach rose help to dry out excess oil and tighten the pores, reducing the appearance of shine and oiliness.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Beach rose cream's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness associated with oily hair conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm scalp irritations that can contribute to oily hair.
Barks: Containing tannins and other astringent compounds, barks help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, making them effective for controlling oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh rosa rugosa petals and dry them in the shade for at least two weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried rosa rugosa petals with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling completely.
Add 1/2 cup of distilled water to the infused oil and mix well before whipping until it becomes light and fluffy.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with oily hair because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps to regulate sebum production in the scalp. This natural ingredient absorbs excess oil and purifies the hair follicles, reducing greasiness and flakiness. Additionally, milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy scalp and calm irritations caused by excessive oil secretion, promoting a healthy and balanced hair environment that looks and feels great. Regular use of these creams can lead to manageable, non-oily locks with a radiant shine.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oiliness by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to oil production.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps associated with oily hair, reducing redness and inflammation.
Saponins: Act as natural emulsifiers, helping to break down and dissolve excess sebum and impurities on the scalp, while also cleansing the pores and removing dirt and debris.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle are used in creams due to their ability to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for oily hair.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing oiliness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry silybum marianum leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area over several days to prevent spoilage.
Combine one part dried silybum marianum herb with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil takes on a dark green color and strong scent.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled oil mixture with an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes until it thickens to a creamy consistency suitable for hair treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn creams helps with oily hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural pH level, reducing excess oil production. The cream's antioxidant-rich properties also help to purify and clarify the hair follicles, allowing for a healthy flow of sebum that is not overproduced. Additionally, sea buckthorn's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness associated with oily hair. Overall, regular use of herbal sea buckthorn cream can help to regulate oil production, leading to healthier-looking hair that is less greasy and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sea buckthorn cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive oil production in the scalp and hair.

Regulatory effects on sebum production: The omega-7 fatty acids present in sea buckthorn cream have been shown to regulate sebum production, helping to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce the appearance of an oily scalp and hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sea buckthorn cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to oil buildup and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sea buckthorn plant contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing oil production and soothing scalp irritation.
Fruits: The fruits of the sea buckthorn plant are rich in essential fatty acids and vitamins, which help to nourish and balance the scalp, while also controlling sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried hippophae rhamnoides berries from trusted sources and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground hippophae rhamnoides powder with 200 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating it slowly over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated after 5 minutes of gentle stirring.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 20 grams of aloe vera gel and stirring well, taking approximately 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, sealing them tightly and storing in a cool dry place for at least 24 hours before use or distribution within the next 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to control the production of excess sebum, thereby reducing oiliness. The cream's gentle yet effective ingredients also help to purify the scalp and absorb excess moisture, leaving your hair feeling clean, refreshed, and manageable. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, making it an excellent natural remedy for oily hair woes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help reduce free radical damage to the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by promoting inflammation and blocking pores.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, reducing oil production and balancing sebum levels.
Natural Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of ginger help tighten the hair follicles and shrink pores, reducing excess oil secretion and minimizing the appearance of oily roots.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help to reduce oil production and soothe scalp irritations.
Buds: Containing essential oils with antiseptic and antibacterial properties, buds help to prevent clogged pores and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one cup of coconut oil and add two tablespoons of grated fresh ginger root.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl over boiling water for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let steep for an additional 30 minutes before straining the oil through cheesecloth.
Combine one cup of beeswax pellets with four tablespoons of coconut oil and one teaspoon of ginger essential oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments stirring between each interval until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The antiseptic qualities of lemongrass also help to reduce dandruff and flakiness, which can contribute to an oily appearance. Additionally, the moisturizing benefits of lemongrass cream provide nourishment without weighing hair down, allowing it to absorb excess oil and leave a refreshing, clean feeling that lasts all day.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemongrass has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness and itchiness, reducing the risk of infections.
Antifungal property: The antifungal properties in lemongrass creams help combat fungal infections, such as dandruff and ringworm, which can exacerbate oily hair issues.
Astringent property: Lemongrass has astringent properties that help dry up excess oil on the scalp, leaving it feeling cleaner and less greasy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing dandruff and itchiness caused by oily hair.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of lemongrass plant contains citral, a compound that helps to control oil production and reduce acne caused by oily skin.
Barks: Lemongrass bark has been traditionally used to treat scalp issues such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema, which can be exacerbated by oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and let it cool at room temperature.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water to the cooled oil and heat it in the double boiler until the wax melts, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it solidify completely before scooping it into small containers for use as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with oily hair because they possess natural astringent and antiseptic properties that work wonders in controlling oil production. The menthol present in peppermint oil helps to dry up excess oil, while its refreshing scent stimulates blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. Additionally, the cream's ability to balance the pH level of the scalp helps to reduce frizz and add shine to oily locks, leaving them looking healthy, manageable, and silky smooth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial agents: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help combat scalp infections, dandruff, and other underlying causes of oily hair, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy scalp.

Cooling and soothing effects: The menthol in peppermint cream provides a cooling sensation that can help calm an itchy or irritated scalp, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with oily hair.

Stimulating circulation: Peppermint's stimulating properties increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps to reduce excess oil production by improving overall circulation and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and soothing irritation caused by oily hair.
Stems: The stems of peppermint contain antioxidants and astringent compounds that help to tighten pores and control excess oil production.
remedy_recipe: Combine one cup of distilled water with one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves in a heatproof bowl.
Steep the peppermint mixture for 15-20 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Melt two tablespoons of coconut oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or saucepan.
Add three drops of peppermint essential oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass containers and allow to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for oily hair are specially formulated products that combine natural ingredients, such as essential oils, extracts, and herbs, to help control excess oil production and soothe scalp irritations. These creams work by balancing the scalp's natural pH, reducing sebum secretion, and absorbing excess moisture, leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. Examples of herbal creams for oily hair include those containing tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile extract. By using these products, individuals with oily hair can enjoy a healthier, more balanced scalp, reduced frizz, and enhanced shine, leading to increased confidence and improved overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal creams is by using ingredients that balance excess oil production. Look for herbs like tea tree, lavender, and chamomile, which have natural antiseptic and astringent properties that help control sebum levels. Apply an herbal cream containing these ingredients directly to the scalp and work it through to the ends of your hair, leaving it on for 30 minutes before shampooing as usual. This will help regulate oil production and leave your hair feeling fresh and manageable.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin barrier function. These natural remedies also address underlying issues such as fungal infections, allergies, and hormonal imbalances that can exacerbate these conditions.
